title: Elegant Umami Korean Flax Hwajeon
description: A creamy and nostalgic Korean dish featuring flax eggs, this ovo-vegetarian-friendly hwajeon is perfect for a special occasion. It combines the richness of umami flavors with the simplicity of a traditional Korean pancake.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the flaxseed meal and water to make flax eggs, letting it sit for 5 minutes to thicken.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, chili paste, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Add the flour mixture to the flax eggs and stir until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Fold in the chopped scallions and crumbled tofu.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and brush with a small amount of oil.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the skillet or griddle.
Cook the hwajeon for 2-3 minutes on the first side, until the edges start to dry and the surface is bubbly.
Loosen the hwajeon with a spatula and flip it over, cooking for an additional 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Broil the hwajeon for 1-2 minutes, until crispy and slightly charred.
Serve the Elegant Umami Korean Flax Hwajeon hot, garnished with additional chopped scallions if desired.
